text.title: pnpm Workspaces with Dependency Catalogs Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns observed in real pnpm catalog adoptions. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: New shared dep added with `pnpm add react -F web@^19.1.2`; hard-coded version drifts from catalog.
antipattern.root-cause: `pnpm add <pkg>@<version>` writes a concrete range, bypassing the catalog.
antipattern.fix: Add catalog entry first; then run `pnpm add react -F web` (no version) — catalog default is picked up.

antipattern.symptom: CI takes 30 min on every commit; only one package changed.
antipattern.root-cause: CI runs `pnpm -r test` on every push.
antipattern.fix: Switch CI to `pnpm --filter '...[origin/main]' test`; cuts time 5–20x.

antipattern.symptom: npm publish rejects `catalog:` / `workspace:*` protocols; release pipeline broken.
antipattern.root-cause: Catalog and workspace protocols are pnpm-only.
antipattern.fix: Add a publish step (e.g., `pnpm publish --no-git-checks` with `--report-summary` flag or a `pnpm-publish-prepublish` script) that replaces catalog/workspace protocols with concrete versions.
